success_factors: Workflow reached terminal success state with all planned nodes executed (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize)
MCP plugin started healthy over stdio and passed plugin-level run checks
Import-first adapter strategy was feasible for this repo (import feasibility 0.72) with low intrusiveness risk
Repository ingestion succeeded despite fallback path (zip fallback), avoiding pipeline abort on upstream analysis tooling failure
failure_reasons: DeepWiki analysis failed (non-blocking), reducing external contextual intelligence
Original project test status is effectively unusable/empty despite a reported 100% coverage field, creating validation blind spots
Generated service metadata is inconsistent (tool_endpoints=0 while endpoint list is very large; LOC/size reported as 0), indicating observability/reporting defects

node_performance.download_time: Completed successfully as part of 238.04s total run; exact per-node timing not captured. Zip fallback likely increased I/O and parsing overhead.
node_performance.analysis_time: Dominant phase given ~1,822 files and deep AST extraction; expected to be the main contributor to total duration.
node_performance.generation_time: File generation succeeded (7 files), likely moderate runtime; however generated artifact metrics (LOC/size/endpoints) were not correctly recorded.
node_performance.test_time: MCP plugin health/run checks completed quickly; original project tests were not meaningfully executed (execution_time=0, no test files).

resource_usage.memory_efficiency: Unknown from telemetry (all zeros), but likely acceptable for this duration; add real memory instrumentation.
resource_usage.cpu_efficiency: Unknown from telemetry (all zeros); AST-heavy analysis on large codebase likely CPU-bound during analysis stage.
resource_usage.disk_usage: Repository is large; zip fallback plus generated outputs increase temporary disk churn. Reported generated size=0 is likely a metric bug.
